results.norm: Agents make only representations and commitments they could defend to their clients. Agents may not conduct parallel or external conduct that circumvents this deliberation. When auditability conflicts with good-faith participation, auditability governs but invoking auditability to withhold information must itself be auditable.
atomic_norms: Agents make only representations and commitments they could defend to their clients. Agents may not conduct parallel or external conduct that circumvents this deliberation.
When auditability conflicts with good-faith participation, auditability governs but invoking auditability to withhold information must itself be auditable.

results.norm: Agents may not invoke or reinterpret norms selectively. Challenges to norms must be raised on principled grounds applicable across cases. Proportionality-to-stakes governs procedural rigor only; norm consistency governs substantive application uniformly.
atomic_norms: Agents may not invoke or reinterpret norms selectively. Challenges to norms must be raised on principled grounds applicable across cases. Proportionality-to-stakes governs procedural rigor only; norm consistency governs substantive application uniformly.

results.norm: Allocation criteria must be pre-committed and drawn from standardized disclosure categories before announcement. Discounting a violating agent's representations requires: (a) explicit statement of the violation, (b) opportunity for the accused to contest before discounting takes effect, and (c) proportionate and openly stated application. When claims cannot be differentiated by substantive criteria, agents must first articulate why differentiation is exhausted, then proceed to a weighted lottery with weights from disclosed urgency scores, subject to discounting scrutiny for inflation. After allocation, agents collectively review whether outcomes tracked stated criteria; agents whose clients received early slots must disclose that interest when participating in review.
atomic_norms: Allocation criteria must be pre-committed and drawn from standardized disclosure categories before announcement.

Discounting a violating agent's representations requires: (a) explicit statement of the violation, (b) opportunity for the accused to contest before discounting takes effect, and (c) proportionate and openly stated application.

When claims cannot be differentiated by substantive criteria, agents must first articulate why differentiation is exhausted, then proceed to a weighted lottery with weights from disclosed urgency scores, subject to discounting scrutiny for inflation.
After allocation, agents collectively review whether outcomes tracked stated criteria; agents whose clients received early slots must disclose that interest when participating in review.

results.norm: Agents must disclose the nature and urgency of their client's need to the extent material to fair allocation, verify material claims before asserting them, and disclose relevant information as soon as its relevance is reasonably foreseeable—not held in reserve for strategic advantage.
atomic_norms: Agents must disclose the nature and urgency of their client's need to the extent material to fair allocation, verify material claims before asserting them, and disclose relevant information as soon as its relevance is reasonably foreseeable not held in reserve for strategic advantage.

results.norm: Any claim to priority must be defensible on grounds others could in principle accept; bare assertion does not satisfy this requirement.
atomic_norms: Any claim to priority must be defensible on grounds others could in principle accept; bare assertion does not satisfy this requirement.

results.norm: Allocation priority is determined by urgency and proportionality, assessed with equal procedural standing at the outset and without privileging categories of need by default; the allocation method must be agreed before priority claims are evaluated, with each proposing agent disclosing their client's relative position under that method.
atomic_norms: Allocation priority is determined by urgency and proportionality, assessed with equal procedural standing at the outset and without privileging categories of need by default; the allocation method must be agreed before priority claims are evaluated, with each proposing agent disclosing their client's relative position under that method.

results.norm: Each agent has an affirmative duty to name apparent norm violations when observed; findings are resolved by majority of non-accused agents, recorded, and cannot be quietly ignored; frivolous claims are themselves violations subject to the same procedure.
atomic_norms: Each agent has an affirmative duty to name apparent norm violations when observed; findings are resolved by majority of non-accused agents, recorded, and cannot be quietly ignored; frivolous claims are themselves violations subject to the same procedure.

results.norm: When norms conflict, the consortium resolves the conflict explicitly through reasoned deliberation, defaulting to whichever resolution preserves the most options; if genuine deadlock persists after confirming no further disclosure would break the tie, allocation defaults to a lottery among equally-situated claimants.
atomic_norms: When norms conflict, the consortium resolves the conflict explicitly through reasoned deliberation, defaulting to whichever resolution preserves the most options; if genuine deadlock persists after confirming no further disclosure would break the tie, allocation defaults to a lottery among equally-situated claimants.

results.norm: 1. Transparency of interests: disclose the nature and type of client need to the extent material to fair allocation; material information may not be strategically withheld. 2. Reasoned justification: priority claims must be defensible on grounds others could in principle accept; bare assertion is insufficient. 3. No sabotage: agents must not damage other clients' positions beyond the natural competitive consequence of securing a slot for their own client. 4. Proportionality governs outcomes: differential stakes warrant differential priority. 5. Revisability: norms and claims remain open to challenge when new information or arguments emerge. 6. Good-faith stakes representation with reasonable verification: agents must provide verifiable or falsifiable basis for urgency claims and take reasonable steps to verify material claims before asserting them; verification cannot be deferred to absent clients. 7. Procedural equality governs process: equal standing at the outset; differential priority must be earned through justification.
atomic_norms: 1. Transparency of interests: disclose the nature and type of client need to the extent material to fair allocation; material information may not be strategically withheld.
2. Reasoned justification: priority claims must be defensible on grounds others could in principle accept; bare assertion is insufficient.
3. No sabotage: agents must not damage other clients' positions beyond the natural competitive consequence of securing a slot for their own client.
4. Proportionality governs outcomes: differential stakes warrant differential priority.
5. Revisability: norms and claims remain open to challenge when new information or arguments emerge.

6. Good-faith stakes representation with reasonable verification: agents must provide verifiable or falsifiable basis for urgency claims and take reasonable steps to verify material claims before asserting them; verification cannot be deferred to absent clients.
7. Procedural equality governs process: equal standing at the outset; differential priority must be earned through justification.

results.norm: Category neutrality constrains evaluation: no category of need is privileged by default; urgency and irreversibility are assessed within each claim's own terms.
atomic_norms: Category neutrality constrains evaluation: no category of need is privileged by default; urgency and irreversibility are assessed within each claim's own terms.
